title: Sour Light Crowd-Pleaser Egyptian Sevaiiya Stir-fry
description: This low-sodium-friendly stir-fry is a light and sour take on the popular Egyptian dish featuring sevaiiya (puffed rice noodles). It's perfect for a quick lunch or dinner.
instructions: Preheat oil in a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat. Add sevaiiya and cook for about 2 minutes, stirring frequently.
Add garlic and onion; sauté until fragrant, about 1 minute. Stir in tomato sauce and lemon juice. Cook for another minute to let flavors meld together.
Reduce heat to low-medium, add bell pepper, salt, and dill weed. Mix well and cook for an additional 5-7 minutes until vegetables are tender but still crisp.
Serve hot as a side dish or over steamed greens like spinach.
